title: heterotheca subaxillaris var. psammophila
intro: Heterotheca subaxillaris var. psammophila, also known as Sand Bunchflower or Sand False Goldenrod, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune system function. The plant's medicinal properties have been utilized in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions like arthritis, fever, and skin infections. The health benefits of Heterotheca subaxillaris var. psammophila are also attributed to its antioxidant and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
